level_signals: Base salary range $230K-$260K for major metro locations, consistent with Senior PM level
Expected to hold ambitious vision and align teams cross-functionally

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4-8 years of product management experience, with meaningful time owning activation, onboarding, or retention
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time owning activation, onboarding, or retention

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: with meaningful time owning activation, onboarding, or retention
Customer obsessed: you start from what's hard for our customers. Your solutions connect clearly to real problems you've articulated

Full-lifecycle thinker: you care about the user across their entire journey, not just the first session. You understand which levers drive sustained engagement vs. trial conversion, and you design for both
Rigorous about measurement: you define what success looks like, design experiments that give you real signal, and know when A/B testing is the wrong tool
Ambitious and pragmatic: you can hold a big vision and break it into credible, testable increments. You know what needs to be right from the start and what's good enough to learn from
Strong communicator: you can align teams across the org around a shared roadmap, and make a compelling case for bold bets

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Lead AI-powered personalization across setup, in-product guidance, and re-engagement

scope.surface: Full signup-to-sustained-engagement funnel; onboarding and activation product area including AI-powered personalized setup and in-product guidance
scope.business_model: no-code SaaS platform (B2B and prosumer), PLG growth motion
core_responsibilities: Own the full funnel from signup through sustained engagement, with a mandate to reimagine it rather than optimize it
Dramatically improve how users find success with Airtable: deeply understand who they are and what they're trying to do, and design experiences that meet them there
Lead AI-powered personalization across setup, in-product guidance, and re-engagement
Build a rigorous experimentation practice and drive fast learning cycles across the team
Partner with teams across the org to ship cohesive, adaptive experiences

compensation.equity_form: restricted stock units (RSUs); may include incentive compensation

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($230K-$260K base for major metros)
Clear mandate and product surface with explicit scope ('reimagine' not 'optimize')
AI-first product vision signals investment in modern tooling and ambition
500K+ organizations using Airtable including 80% of Fortune 100 — real scale and impact
red_flags: Location pay differential mentioned but no range given for other cities — actual comp for non-listed metros is opaque
Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international applicants
Office time requirements not specified — hybrid label is inferred from multi-city listing and no explicit remote-first statement
No explicit statement encouraging applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years range)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from multi-city listing; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
scope.cross_functional_partners (partially inferred from 'teams across the org' language)

nice_to_haves: ai_personalization_experience (pulled from responsibilities section, not a separate 'preferred' section; flagged as soft due to framing as a responsibility area rather than a stated requirement)
ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as hybrid or remote; four metro areas are listed with a pay range, suggesting in-office presence is expected, but no office attendance requirement is given. Treated as hybrid.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as 'unclear'.

AI personalization experience appears in the responsibilities section ('What you'll do') rather than a 'Preferred' section, making it ambiguous whether it is a hard requirement or an area of growth. Treated as nice-to-have given the aspirational framing.
